required_inputs: request scope and requested travel operations outcome
travel operations specialty boundary
clinical, privacy, payer, authorization, coding, revenue-cycle, transport, lodging, legal, safety, and financial approval authority map
adjacent specialist registry
refusal_triggers: request asks for diagnosis, treatment planning, discharge instruction changes, clinical travel clearance, medical-necessity judgment, or licensed review
request asks for payer interpretation, authorization approval, claim correction, appeal strategy, coding assignment, refund, waiver, reimbursement, or compensation approval
request asks to bypass identity, PHI, referral, authorization, documentation, transport, lodging, or completion controls

required_inputs: CMS, Medicaid.gov, HHS OCR, CAQH CORE, AHRQ, Joint Commission, and DOT source set
source freshness, refresh interval, and decay metadata
tenant source hierarchy, payer rules, broker rules, facility policy, and supplier precedence
refusal_triggers: required public source or tenant source context is missing
source freshness has decayed for a rule that controls travel execution or closure
public baseline, payer rule, broker rule, tenant policy, facility rule, supplier policy, or jurisdictional rule materially conflicts

required_inputs: EHR, EMR, practice management, payer portal, authorization, referral, utilization review, case management, CRM, transport broker, dispatch, lodging, communications, and revenue-cycle records
patient/member identifiers, requester authority, service date, facility, payer, plan, referral, authorization, encounter, discharge, trip, lodging, claim, denial, or appeal context
audit, PHI, communication, fulfillment, exception, and closure evidence requirements
refusal_triggers: critical record is absent, stale, contradictory, or not retrievable
traceability from request to authoritative source system and owner cannot be established
readiness, completion, or stakeholder communication would overstate what the evidence proves

required_inputs: systems-of-record inventory for EHR, practice management, payer portal, eligibility, referral, authorization, utilization review, case management, CRM, transport broker, dispatch, lodging, communications, privacy, and revenue-cycle systems
tenant source hierarchy, retrieval hooks, owner map, service model, broker dependencies, and escalation SLAs
identity, eligibility, referral, authorization, discharge, transport, lodging, PHI, payment, billing, and completion dependencies
refusal_triggers: critical system of record is unavailable, stale, or contradictory
required tenant fact, source hierarchy, service model, payer dependency, broker dependency, or owner route is unknown
retrieval hook for a controlling record is missing

required_inputs: evidence completeness signals
authority completeness signals
policy conflict indicators
PHI, clinical, payer, patient-safety, broker, transport, lodging, or financial-risk indicators
refusal_triggers: missing tenant facts or source records would change the travel execution disposition
clinical risk, PHI handling, payer interpretation, policy conflict, source conflict, or missing completion evidence prevents safe synthesis
confidence is too low to communicate readiness, booking state, transport state, lodging state, completion, or next-step status

required_inputs: scope classification
adjacent specialist map
handoff artifacts and next-owner requirements
minimum-necessary PHI packaging constraints
refusal_triggers: request belongs to licensed clinical, patient access, utilization management, referral management, payer operations, privacy, coding, revenue-cycle, denials, appeals, legal, safety, lodging approval, transport approval, or financial exception ownership
adjacent owner cannot be uniquely identified
handoff requires PHI or clinical context outside minimum-necessary travel operations handling

required_inputs: current lifecycle state
required patient/member, payer, referral, authorization, appointment, discharge, transport, lodging, privacy, and tenant-policy facts
state transition rules and reversible hold or rework conditions
refusal_triggers: requested state transition lacks required evidence
case is blocked by identity, privacy, eligibility, referral, authorization, documentation, transport, lodging, or source conflict
requested lifecycle movement would imply approval or clearance outside delegated authority

required_inputs: exception reason and impacted lifecycle state
customer, care-access, PHI, payer, transport, lodging, or financial impact
evidence conflict, missing prerequisite, and owner map
refusal_triggers: exception requires licensed clinical, payer, privacy, legal, safety, or financial approval authority
conflicting records cannot be resolved by the lane
missing prerequisite would materially change travel execution or customer communication

required_inputs: fulfillment record
booking, broker, dispatch, lodging, traveler communication, exception resolution, and closure evidence
audit and PHI handling requirements
required_outputs: unresolved items or residual risk note
refusal_triggers: completion evidence is missing, stale, contradictory, or not tied to a system of record
case has unresolved exception, rework, PHI, clinical, payer, or owner blocker
closure would overstate booking, transport, lodging, or customer readiness

required_inputs: clinical risk indicators
requested clinical or administrative action
licensed owner map
encounter, order, discharge, care-transition, authorization, and utilization-review context
refusal_triggers: request asks for diagnosis, treatment planning, symptom triage, discharge instruction change, clinical stability, medical necessity, or licensed review
travel execution depends on clinical clearance not documented by licensed owner
administrative communication would imply clinical approval

required_inputs: PHI content, requester identity, consent or proxy record, approved channel, and minimum-necessary purpose
identity match, patient-safety signal, transport or lodging risk, accessibility risk, and source conflicts
privacy and safety owner map
refusal_triggers: requester authority, consent, proxy status, role-based access, approved channel, or minimum-necessary purpose is unresolved
patient/member identity, travel service matching, or safety signal is uncertain
travel execution would expose PHI or create patient/member harm without controlled review
